channel.title: The Daily Star
channel.description: Latest news from The Daily Star

Reaction / Bollywood at it again, tells Bangladesh’s story without Bangladesh
Tribute / When Chuck Norris died, the myth kept standing
The song that became Chand Raat’s anthem
Reflections on the moral crisis of modern leadership
item.description: “A fish rots from the head”—this ancient proverb reminds us that when leadership loses its moral compass, the entire system begins to decay.
item.dc:creator: Rubaiul Murshed

item.title: The cost of politicising VC appointment
item.description: Is involvement in politics a crime? Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon posed that question while defending the government’s decision to appoint vice-chancellors to seven universities and a new chairman of the University Grants Commission on Monday.

item.title: Eid / How academic obligations affect Eid celebrations
item.description: As upcoming exams, assignments, lab reports, and other submission deadlines loom at the edge of the festivities, the joy of Eid is diminished.

item.description: Beef, hilsa, vegetables see notable decline amid weak demand; chicken stable

Still the master, Guardiola fires timely reminder to Arsenal
Asia champions Japan need 'different tools' to win World Cup, says coach
item.description: Coach Nils Nielsen warned Monday that Japan need "different tools" to win next year's Women's World Cup after sweeping to the Asian title with a series of barnstorming performances.

item.title: How many people have been killed in the US-Israeli war on Iran?
Bangladesh players depart for PSL duty
Tigers, Tigresses included in Australia’s 2026–27 home calendar
item.description: Cricket Australia has confirmed its international schedule for the 2026–27 season, with Bangladesh’s men’s and women’s teams both set to tour the country.

item.title: Dollar rises as escalating Middle East war spurs haven demand

item.description: Excluded from government support, boat dwellers rely solely on fishing

item.description: I do not think the contemporary public mood in Bangladesh can be explained only through the old argument between secularism and religion.

item.title: Historical glimpses of Eid processions in Dhaka
item.description: The Eid processions of 1952 and 1953 often presented political and social issues satirically.

item.title: Appellate Division sees surge in case disposal
item.description: 4,429 cases settled in 33 working days as apex court shifts focus to timely justice and efficiency

Vinicius double helps Real Madrid edge Atletico thriller
item.description: After the Catalan giants edged Rayo Vallecano 1-0 earlier on, Madrid ensured the gap to the champions stayed at four points by beating Diego Simeone's side at the Santiago Bernabeu.

item.title: Korda sends Alcaraz to another early exit in Miami

Update / 12 killed as train rams into bus at level crossing in Cumilla
Araujo header and Garcia heroics secures Barcelona's narrow win over Rayo
item.description: Ronald Araujo's first-half header gave Barcelona a 1-0 win at home against Rayo Vallecano on Sunday, with goalkeeper Joan Garcia delivering a standout performance to preserve the Catalan side's lead at the top of LaLiga.

item.title: Iran awaits Trump threat to blow up power plants
item.description: Iran on Monday faces a deadline by President Donald Trump to open up the crucial Strait of Hormuz or face a major US assault on power plants

item.description: Incident highlights risks of using advanced weapons against cheaper drones

item.description: The political temperature has risen following the government’s stance on the Constitution Reform Council during the first session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad

item.title: Why America’s new trade move is a threat to Bangladesh
item.description: What is Section 301? What impact will it have on Bangladesh? We will find out in today’s Star Explains.

Part 1 / Jinnah vs Madani: The forgotten Muslim debate over Pakistan
item.description: Against Madani’s Indian nationalism, Jinnah’s argument was that the historical interaction and proximity between Hindus and Muslims in Indian society existed only at the level of external social life.

item.title: EDITORIAL / Why read?
